song.title: Oh, How I Love Jesus
song.author: Fredrick Whitfield
song.presentation: V1 C V2 C V3 C V4 C E
song.lyrics: [v1] 1) There is a name I love to hear, I love to sing its worth; It sounds like music in mine ear, The sweetest name on earth. [c] Chorus Oh, how I love Jesus, Oh, how I love Jesus, Oh, how I love Jesus, Because He first loved me! [v2] 2) It tells me of a Savior’s love, Who died to set me free; It tells me of His precious blood, The sinner’s perfect plea. [v3] 3) It tells me what my Father hath, In store for every day, And though I tread a darksome path, Yields sunshine all the way. [v4] 4) It tells of One whose loving heart Can feel my deepest woe, Who in each sorrow bears a part, That none can bear below. [E] 1) There is a name I love to hear, I love to sing its worth; It sounds like music in mine ear, The sweetest name on earth. Chorus Oh, how I love Jesus, Oh, how I love Jesus, Oh, how I love Jesus, Because He first loved me! 2) It tells me of a Savior’s love, Who died to set me free; It tells me of His precious blood, The sinner’s perfect plea. 3) It tells me what my Father hath, In store for every day, And though I tread a darksome path, Yields sunshine all the way. 4) It tells of One whose loving heart Can feel my deepest woe, Who in each sorrow bears a part, That none can bear below.
